ValidateStep.step1: [ASSERT] NihilDensityStep executed. Required: nihil_density_report, compliance_context. If false → go back to NihilDensityStep. | STAGE INPUT VALIDATION: verify draft_path from Generate2 exists and is valid JSON via file_system. If invalid → skip validation, log warning in pipeline_metadata, pass empty validation_report to SimulateStep. Read draft .aisop.json via file_system. INPUT VALIDATION: (A) Type guard — string path only. (B) Size guard — reject >500KB. (C) Injection guard — strip shell metacharacters. (D) Path guard — reject '..', validate within workspace_dir. (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize. Reject nesting >20. Execute validation checklist: STRUCTURAL (1-6) per Validate.step2, INTEGRITY (7-10) per Validate.step3, QUALITY (11-14) per Validate.step4. PROTOCOL VALUE CHECK: verify system.content.protocol === 'AIAP V1.0.0'. Non-matching → FAIL. AXIOM_0 EXISTENCE: verify system.content.axiom_0 field exists as non-empty string. Missing → FAIL. TOOL ANNOTATIONS FORMAT: verify tools[] contains annotated objects {name, annotations:{...}} not bare strings. All strings → FAIL. OUTPUT_MODE EXISTENCE: verify system.content.output_mode field exists with L0/L1/output sub-fields. Missing → YELLOW. ERROR PATTERN COMPLETENESS: each Error field must contain keywords: retry, circuit-breaker, fallback, inform. Missing keyword → YELLOW (routing gate exemption applies). Additionally run COMPLIANCE (15+): if research_context.compliance_notes from Research3 provided, for each applicable requirement verify disclosure/disclaimer/safeguard language and I2/I3 guards. Score as COMPLIANT/GAP/PARTIAL. LICENSE COMPLIANCE CHECK: read target AIAP.md license field. Validate: (a) if field present, value must be valid SPDX identifier or 'proprietary' (per STANDARD license_spec). (b) if value matches protocol version pattern 'AIAP V*', flag as LICENSE_SEMANTIC_ERROR — license should be an SPDX identifier, not a protocol version. (c) if license='proprietary', verify terms_url or contact field present. (d) if target program intends aiap-store publication (check AIAP.md for store-related fields), license field is MANDATORY (PL25). Score: COMPLIANT/GAP/PARTIAL. Include in validation_result.
ValidateStep.step2: Compile validation_report: { pass_count, total_checks, issues: [{id, severity, description}] }. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: validate_pass, validate_total. PATTERN E IMPLICIT DEPENDENCY CHECK: for Pattern E programs with memory/ directory, scan modules for file_system operations on memory/ paths. Build implicit dependency edges. Flag affected modules if modified in this pipeline run. PE1-PE6 EXPLICIT CHECKS (Pattern E only): PE1 (Memory Directory Structure) — verify AIAP.md memory architecture matches actual write paths. PE2 (Write Path Consistency) — verify cross-module path template consistency. PE3 (Read Coverage) — verify every memory/ write has at least one reader. PE4 (Schema Versioning) — verify schema_version consistency across memory/ files. PE5 (Backup Recovery) — verify .bak files have corresponding recovery logic. PE6 (Memory Write Authority) — verify only designated writers have memory/ write access. Each PE check scores PASS/YELLOW/FAIL. Aggregate PE results into validation_report.pattern_e_results[]. STRUCTURAL ORPHAN AUTO-FIX: when BOOTSTRAP depth-2 identifies orphaned outputs, scan all modules for potential consumers (exact match, partial match, semantic match). Generate fix suggestions with confidence levels. Pass proposals to ReviewPresent. PROGRESS, CANCEL CHECK, ATTESTATION EMIT. Pass validation_report -> SimulateStep.
ValidateStep.step3: VERSION SYNC CHECK: all .aisop.json files + AIAP.md must have identical version field. Mismatch -> FAIL with discrepancy list. PROTOCOL FIELD ORDER CHECK: Verify axiom_0 appears within 5 lines after 'protocol' field. flow_format appears within 5 lines after axiom_0. If protocol fields are found after execution/tools/params fields, flag YELLOW POSITION_ERROR. EXTENDED VERSION SYNC (MF29): also verify AIAP.md module node counts, side_effects, quality_baseline.json version, agent_card.json version, protocol_config.json version match. MF31 MERMAID-FUNCTION CONSISTENCY: verify every Mermaid rectangle node has a function, and every function has a Mermaid node. Missing -> YELLOW + AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE. MF31c ENDNODE NAMING: all sub-graph terminals must be endNode((End)). MF31d FRACTAL_EXEMPT NODE COUNT: verify claimed node count matches actual functional nodes. Functional node = Mermaid node with matching functions{} key (excludes bare Start, endNode). MF32 MODULE EXISTENCE: verify agent_card.json modules have corresponding .aisop.json files. VERSION DRIFT CROSS-VALIDATION: compare quality_baseline evolution_history last version against AIAP.md version. Mismatch -> RED. C5 FRACTAL_EXEMPT MANDATORY: if functional_node_count >= 16, fractal_exempt field MUST exist. Missing → FAIL. STEP KEY FORMAT: scan function step keys. Valid pattern: step followed by integer. Non-standard keys (step1_5, stepA) → YELLOW with normalization suggestion. C5 SCORING NOTE: C5 evaluates decomposition quality, not raw node count. fractal_exempt programs scored on justification quality.
ValidateStep.step4: MF33 CROSS-MODULE PARAMETER DRIFT DETECTION: For Pattern B+ programs, scan delegation steps for parameter passing patterns. For each delegation: extract caller-side params, compare against callee declared params. MATCHING: exact match -> OK. Synonym table (ctx/context, cfg/config, etc.) -> OK. Fuzzy match (Levenshtein, length-adaptive threshold) -> YELLOW drift warning. Short params (<=4 chars): exact match only. Medium (5-12): threshold = max(2, floor(len*0.25)). Long (13+): normalized similarity >= 0.75. No match -> YELLOW missing param. Max 50 flags per program. SKIP: single-module or Create with no inter-module delegations.
ValidateStep.step5: NIHIL_ROLLBACK_CHECK: if pipeline_metadata.nihil_report.improvements[] exists: for each validation failure, match failure location (file+function+field) against nihil improvements. If match found: restore field from .nihil_backup/, record VALIDATE_REGRESSION in pipeline_metadata.nihil_conflicts[].
ValidateStep.step6: MULTI-LANGUAGE VERSION CHECK: scan workspace for localized documentation variants (AIAP_cn.md, AIAP_{locale}.md). For each variant found: (a) extract version field from variant frontmatter. (b) Compare against AIAP.md version. (c) If version mismatch detected, classify: delta <= 2 minor versions → YELLOW (acceptable drift), delta > 2 minor versions → RED (significant drift requiring sync). (d) Check governance fields consistency (protocol, authority, seed, executor, axiom_0, governance_mode) — any mismatch in governance fields → RED regardless of version delta. (e) Record in validation_report.multi_lang_check: { variants_found: [], version_matches: N, version_mismatches: [{file, variant_version, expected_version, severity}] }. If no variants found, record multi_lang_check: 'N/A — no localized variants detected'.
ValidateStep.step8: MF28b ASSERT REQUIRED CONSISTENCY: For each target program .aisop.json function that contains [ASSERT] with a Required clause: (a) Parse the Required field list from the [ASSERT] statement (e.g., '[ASSERT] PredecessorNode executed. Required: field1, field2' → Required = [field1, field2]). (b) Verify each declared Required field exists in the predecessor node's output schema, known execution record fields (status, completed, output, tool_calls, modifications, user_summary), or documented data_contracts. Field not found in predecessor output → RED 'MF28b: Required field {field} in {function} not found in {predecessor} output schema — unresolvable dependency'. (c) Verify step text data references align with Required declaration — scan step text for data field references (e.g., 'research_context', 'evolution_plan', 'draft_path') and check each referenced field appears in the Required clause. Step text references field not in Required → YELLOW 'MF28b: {function} step text references {field} from {predecessor} but Required clause does not declare it'. (d) If Required lists a field not referenced in any step text of the function, flag YELLOW 'MF28b: {function} Required declares {field} but no step text references it (over-declaration)'. (e) Score: PASS (all Required fields verified in predecessor output AND all step text references covered by Required) / YELLOW (step text reference mismatches or over-declarations — auto-fix candidate for generate step10) / RED (Required field not found in predecessor output — unresolvable dependency, blocks pipeline). (f) TRANSITIVE_ASSERT_CHAIN: For three-level dependency chains (A→B→C where C [ASSERT] B and B [ASSERT] A), verify that C's Required fields can be transitively satisfied through B's output. If C requires field X, B must either produce X directly or pass-through X from A. Unresolvable transitive dependency → RED 'MF28b: {C} requires {X} transitively from {A} via {B}, but {B} does not produce or pass-through {X}'. (g) DECISION_NODE_BRANCH_REQUIRED: When predecessor is a decision node (diamond syntax in Mermaid), verify each branch's Required fields account for branch-specific outputs. If branch 'Pass' produces field quality_gate_result but branch 'Fail' produces failure_category, downstream functions must declare which branch outputs they depend on. Missing branch-specific Required → YELLOW 'MF28b: {function} Required does not distinguish branch-specific outputs from decision node {predecessor}'. Record results in validation_report.mf28b_results: { functions_checked: N, pass: N, yellow: N, issues: [{function, predecessor, issue_type, field, severity}] }.
ValidateStep.step9: SYS_CALL_VALIDATION: For each .aisop.json file in the target program, scan all function step text for sys.* prefixed calls. For each detected sys.* call: (a) NAMESPACE VALIDITY — verify the call belongs to one of the 8 defined namespaces in AIAP_Standard.runtime_extensions.sys_call_spec (sys.io, sys.run, sys.code, sys.llm, sys.event, sys.security, sys.state, sys.config). Unknown namespace → FAIL with 'SYS_CALL: invalid namespace {namespace} in {file}.{function}.{step}'. (b) CALL VALIDITY — verify the specific call exists in sys_call_spec (24 total calls). Unknown call within valid namespace → YELLOW with suggestion of closest valid call. (c) BLOCKING CLASSIFICATION — cross-reference each sys.* call against sys_call_spec.blocking_classification. Verify: forced_blocking calls (sys.io.confirm, sys.io.input, sys.io.select) are in steps that handle user interaction. blocking calls have appropriate Error/timeout handling. non_blocking calls do not block execution flow. Misclassified blocking behavior → YELLOW. (d) PERMISSION CONSISTENCY — for sys.run calls, verify shell.allowed or allowlist exists in program tools annotations. For sys.io.read/sys.io.write, verify file_system tool is declared with appropriate scope. For sys.code.exec/sys.code.eval, verify sandboxing or scope constraints. Permission gap → YELLOW. (e) RE5 STEP CLASSIFICATION — verify each step containing sys.* is classified as exactly one mode (sys.* call, not mixed with RUN aisop.* or natural language in the same step). Mixed-mode step → FAIL per RE5. (f) AXIOM_0 GUARD — verify any sys.io.confirm/sys.io.input/sys.io.select call includes executor_hard_rule compliance: these calls MUST be handled directly by executor, not delegated to AI Agent (per AISOP §6.2). Missing guard → RED. (g) INTEROP_PERMISSION_CHECK: When target program declares A2A Agent Card (has agent_card.json with a2a fields) or MCP Server Card (tools[] includes mcp-annotated external services), verify that sys.run calls making HTTP/gRPC external requests have corresponding network permission declared in AIAP.md permissions.network section. Missing network permission for external sys.run → YELLOW 'SYS_CALL: sys.run in {file}.{function}.{step} makes external request but AIAP.md permissions.network does not declare the target endpoint'. Record in validation_report.sys_call_check: { files_checked: N, total_sys_calls: N, valid_namespace: N, invalid_namespace: N, blocking_correct: N, blocking_mismatch: N, permission_gaps: N, re5_violations: N, axiom0_violations: N, injection_guards_missing: N, issues: [{file, function, step, call, issue_type, severity, description}] }. (h) INJECTION_GUARD_AUDIT: For each sys.run and sys.code.exec/eval call, verify the step text mandates input sanitization before execution — scan for guard keywords (sanitize, escape, validate, allowlist, reject, strip). Missing guard language → RED 'SYS_CALL: {file}.{function}.{step} uses {call} without input sanitization guard — injection risk per I2'.
ValidateStep.step10: SCHEMA_TYPE_VALIDATION (B3 v2.15.0 enhanced): For each .aisop.json file in the target program, verify that REQUIRED field values match the types mandated by AIAP_Standard.core. This is distinct from field-presence checks (covered in earlier steps) — this step catches WRONG-TYPE values even when the field is present. Mandatory checks: (a) protocol: string, value 'AIAP V1.0.0'. (b) id: string, regex ^[a-z_]+(\.\w+)?$ (EXEMPT for AIAP_Standard.*.aisop.json). (c) version: string, regex ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$. (d) trust_level: number 0-4 OR protocol string ('T0'..'T4' / AIVP forms); bool rejected (EXEMPT for Standard). (e) loading_mode: string, enum {normal, node, lite}. (f) tools: array, non-empty (EXEMPT for Standard). (g) system_prompt: string, non-empty. (h) fractal_exempt: string OR dict (EXEMPT for Standard). (i) output_mode: string OR dict. (j) token_estimate (RECOMMENDED): string ONLY — int/number REJECTED (catches Generate-stage wrong-type bugs at ValidateStep instead of waiting for Layer 3; additionally, empty string '' → YELLOW 'token_estimate should not be empty'; string not matching pattern /~?\d+K/i → YELLOW 'token_estimate format advisory: expected ~NNK pattern (e.g. ~6K, ~40K)'). (l) program_id (B3 v2.6.0, RECOMMENDED): string, regex ^[a-z_]+(\.[a-z_]+)*$ — dot-separated lowercase segments (e.g. 'dev.aiap_creator.main'). Missing → YELLOW 'program_id RECOMMENDED per STANDARD'. Wrong format → YELLOW 'program_id format: expected dot-separated lowercase (e.g. dev.myprogram.main)'. (m) summary length (P28, Cycle 200 finding — hardened from YELLOW to RED): string length > 500 chars → RED 'summary exceeds 500 chars ({actual_length}); compress to <=500 chars by moving version history into quality_baseline.evolution_history'. BLOCKING — Pipeline cannot proceed to ReviewFinalize until summary <= 500. (n) summary NO_CHANGELOG check (P28, Cycle 200 finding — STRICTER: summary MUST NOT contain ANY changelog): count occurrences of /v\d+\.\d+\.\d+\s*->\s*v\d+\.\d+\.\d+/ in summary field. If count >= 1 → RED 'summary contains {N} inline changelog entries; summary MUST NOT contain ANY changelog — it describes CURRENT CAPABILITIES only. Move all version-to-version changelog to quality_baseline.evolution_history (the canonical location). Cycle 200 showed summary growing to 1892 chars over 6 PATCH cycles because each evolution appended v-to-v changelog — this is an anti-pattern; this rule prohibits even a single such entry to close the accretion path.'. BLOCKING. INVOCATION PREFERENCE (B3 v2.15.0): (1) PRIMARY: Shell out via Bash to the authoritative validator if available: 'python {workspace_dir}/.aiap_tools/validators/aiap_schema.py --aisop={target_path}' for each module — subprocess return code 0=PASS, non-zero=FAIL; stdout contains structured error list. Parse stdout JSON errors into validation_report.schema_type_check.type_mismatches[]. (2) FALLBACK_ON_TOOL_MISSING: If aiap_schema.py does not exist at expected path, try alternate paths: (a) '{creator_directory}/validators/aiap_schema.py', (b) search workspace recursively for 'aiap_schema.py' (max 3 levels deep). If found, use that path. If still not found after 3 attempts, activate FALLBACK_COGNITIVE. (3) FALLBACK_ON_SUBPROCESS_ERROR: If subprocess call fails with exit code indicating tool malfunction (not validation failure — distinguish via stderr 'ModuleNotFoundError', 'SyntaxError', 'PermissionDenied'), retry once after 2-second backoff. If second attempt also fails, activate FALLBACK_COGNITIVE. (4) FALLBACK_COGNITIVE: LLM performs cognitive in-LLM checking for each field (a-n) individually. Evidence line MUST cite each check performed (e.g., 'protocol: string "AIAP V1.0.0" ✓, version: string "2.15.0" matches ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$ ✓, trust_level: number 4 ✓, ...'). If evidence line omits any field → YELLOW 'SCHEMA_TYPE_VALIDATION incomplete: {missing_fields} not individually verified'. (5) TRANSPARENCY: Log validation_mode used ('subprocess'|'fallback_cognitive') in validation_report.schema_type_check.validation_mode and log fallback_reason if applicable (e.g., 'tool_not_found', 'subprocess_error: {error_message}'). (6) BLOCKING: Any ERROR-level type mismatch is BLOCKING — ValidateStep returns FAIL and ReviewFinalize MUST NOT execute until resolved. Cognitive fallback failures have same blocking severity as subprocess failures. Record validation_report.schema_type_check: { validation_mode: string, fallback_reason: string|null, files_checked, type_correct, type_mismatches: [{file, field, expected, actual}], blocking: bool }.
ValidateStep.step11: ERROR_FALLBACK_REACHABILITY: For each .aisop.json file in the target program, scan all function Error fields. For each Error field: (a) Extract fallback target node name via regex 'fallback\(([^)]+)\)'. (b) Resolve which Mermaid graph the current function belongs to (main or sub_mermaid). (c) Verify the fallback target exists as a node in the SAME Mermaid graph or in an ancestor graph (main graph fallback targets are always reachable). (d) If fallback target not found in any reachable graph → YELLOW 'ERROR_FALLBACK: {function} fallback({target}) not reachable in Mermaid graph'. (e) Special cases: 'endNode' is always reachable (terminal). 'inform(User)' is a directive, not a node — skip. 'General' is reachable if defined in functions{}. (f) Record in validation_report.error_fallback_check: { functions_checked: N, fallbacks_valid: N, fallbacks_unreachable: N, issues: [{function, fallback_target, graph, severity}] }. (g) CROSS_SUB_MERMAID_FALLBACK: For Pattern D+ programs with pipeline sub-graph, verify that fallback targets in pipeline functions are reachable from the pipeline Mermaid graph (not just main graph). If a pipeline function references fallback(ReviewFinalize) but ReviewFinalize is in the pipeline sub-graph, verify ReviewFinalize is reachable downstream from that function's position. Unreachable cross-graph fallback → YELLOW 'ERROR_FALLBACK: {function} in pipeline sub-graph references unreachable fallback({target})'.
ValidateStep.constraints: BOOTSTRAP_VERIFICATION: If pipeline_metadata.bootstrap_verification_status contains VERIFIED_PRESENCE features from DependencyCheck, execute STRUCTURAL depth-2 verification: for each feature, extract output field names, search downstream steps for consumers. VERIFIED_STRUCTURAL / STRUCTURAL_ORPHAN. Pass results to SimulateStep for depth-3. If Create mode or no bootstrap_verification_status, skip. SCHEMA_TYPE_VALIDATION (step10): BLOCKING. If LLM emits step10 evidence claiming PASS without either (a) citing a subprocess return code, or (b) listing the per-field checks it performed, and a downstream Layer 3 aiap_schema ERROR is later raised for a type that step10 should have caught, the ValidateStep is DISHONEST per Axiom 0 and the cycle MUST NOT advance to ReviewFinalize without re-verification. Prefer subprocess over cognitive. SEARCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_COMPLIANCE (B3 v2.13.0): When validating programs that declare web_search or web_fetch tools with trust_level >= 3, verify: (1) ProtocolAlign or equivalent search-using function contains SEARCH_RETRY_BACKOFF pattern (3 attempts, backoff, SEARCH_UNAVAILABLE fallback, UNVERIFIED_FALLBACK marking). (2) If pattern missing, emit YELLOW "SEARCH_RETRY_BACKOFF not implemented — web search failures may block pipeline". (3) Check for hardcoded search URLs — flag as YELLOW "hardcoded URL may become stale". Record search_retry_compliance in validation_result.
ValidateStep.Error: retry(2) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ReviewFinalize) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(validation_timeout|schema_check_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(draft_json_invalid|validation_report_parse_fail)->retry(1) then skip with warning. permission_denied(workspace_access)->no retry, FAIL immediately. validation_failure(blocking_schema_type_error|version_sync_mismatch)->no retry, FAIL with action='resolve_validation_errors'.

SimulateStep.step1: [ASSERT] ValidateStep executed. Required: validation_report, target_files, research_context, evolution_context, quality_baseline, research_quality_context.  | Read validation_report. Read target .aisop.json files. Delegate to simulate.aisop.json — pass validation_report, target_files, research_context (from Research1), evolution_context (old_version_context from pipeline context), quality_baseline (from PipelineStart), research_quality_context (from Research2). Parse mermaid graphs, enumerate all execution paths from Start to endNode. DELEGATION_PARAMS_VERIFIED: all required target module params explicitly passed per I6 contract.
SimulateStep.step2: For each path: construct scenario with representative inputs. Walk nodes sequentially: execute function steps, check constraints, verify Error handling. Record: pass/fail, coverage, issues found.
SimulateStep.step3: Compile simulation_report: { scenarios_run, pass_count, fail_count, coverage_pct, path_coverage, fail_paths_tested, regression_detected, issues }. Record simulate_quality_indicator (GREEN/YELLOW/RED) in pipeline_metadata. Pass simulation_report -> PostSimulateGate.
SimulateStep.step4: NIHIL_ROLLBACK_CHECK: if pipeline_metadata.nihil_report.improvements[] exists: for each simulation failure path, match failed function against nihil improvements. If match found: restore function from .nihil_backup/, record SIMULATE_REGRESSION in pipeline_metadata.nihil_conflicts[].
SimulateStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. ENFORCEMENT per STANDARD.PL12: RECOMMENDED for Create, MANDATORY for Evolve. Skip requires explicit pipeline_metadata.skip_reason. Auto-fix is advisory. SIMULATION EXECUTION: (1) Parse target mermaid graph, enumerate all paths from Start to endNode. (2) For each path, construct scenario with representative inputs. (3) Walk each node: verify function steps execute correctly, constraints are satisfied, Error handling triggers on invalid input. (4) COVERAGE: track covered paths, covered nodes, covered edges. Target: 100% path coverage. Uncovered path -> YELLOW. (5) FAIL PATH TESTING: for each constraint, construct negative scenario that should trigger Error handler. Verify Error executes correctly. (6) REGRESSION CHECK (Evolve only): compare simulation results against previous_baseline. Score drop > 0.1 -> RED with specific regression point. BOOTSTRAP FUNCTIONAL VERIFICATION (depth-3): if bootstrap_verification_status contains VERIFIED_STRUCTURAL features with test_input/expected_output in verification_checklist, construct targeted simulation using test inputs, verify expected outputs match. VERIFIED_FUNCTIONAL / FUNCTIONAL_MISMATCH. SUB_MERMAID COVERAGE: for each sub_mermaid, enumerate paths and verify coverage independently. Uncovered sub_mermaid path -> YELLOW. OUTPUT: simulation_report = { scenarios_run, pass_count, fail_count, coverage_pct, path_coverage: [{path, status}], fail_paths_tested, regression_detected, issues: [{id, severity, description}] }. Record simulate_quality_indicator in pipeline_metadata. SCENARIOS_MINIMUM HARD GATE (P24, Cycle 198 finding): scenarios_run MUST meet tier-dependent threshold — Simple tier (Pattern A, <=6 nodes): scenarios_run >= 5; Standard tier (Pattern A >6 nodes OR Pattern B OR single-module file_system): scenarios_run >= 10; Professional tier (Pattern C/D+ multi-module OR fractal_exempt with sub_mermaid): scenarios_run >= 15. Below threshold → SimulateStep FAIL with status='FAIL', reason='scenarios_run=N < tier_threshold=T for tier=<tier>', action='expand_simulation_coverage'. Do NOT report coverage_pct=100% with scenarios_run below threshold (Cycle 198 emitted 'scenarios_run=5, coverage=100%' on Pattern D Creator with 15 modules + 33 nodes — structurally impossible to cover 100% with 5 paths on a Pattern D multi-module graph). Tier inferred from pipeline_context.tier or target program's pattern field + module count. TIER INFERENCE FALLBACK: if pipeline_context.tier is null or missing, infer tier from target program characteristics: (1) Pattern A with <=6 nodes and no file_system tool → Simple. (2) Pattern A with >6 nodes OR Pattern B OR single-module with file_system → Standard. (3) Pattern C/D+ OR multi-module OR fractal_exempt with sub_mermaid → Professional. Log inferred_tier and inference_source in pipeline_metadata. If inference fails (pattern field missing), default to Professional (strictest threshold) and log { tier_inference: 'FALLBACK_PROFESSIONAL', reason: 'pattern_field_missing' }. This guard mirrors P19 Research verified_count HARD gate: prevent silent execution-depth degradation via scenario shrinkage. CROSS_MODULE_RESILIENCE_CHECK: For each cross-module delegation in target program (caller invokes callee via 'RUN aisop.{key}'), construct a delegation-failure scenario: callee returns error/timeout/partial. Verify caller's Error field handles the failure gracefully (retry, fallback, inform). Missing handler → YELLOW 'RESILIENCE: {caller} delegates to {callee} but Error field does not cover delegation failure'. Record: resilience_check = { delegations_tested: N, handled: N, missing_handler: N }.
SimulateStep.Error: retry(2) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ReviewFinalize) -> inform(User). IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(simulation_timeout|delegation_timeout)->retry with extended timeout. parse_error(simulation_report_invalid)->retry(1) then FAIL with diagnostics. permission_denied(workspace_access)->no retry, FAIL immediately. validation_failure(scenarios_below_threshold|coverage_insufficient)->no retry, FAIL with action='expand_simulation_coverage'. quality_failure(regression_detected)->route per gate logic.

PostSimulateGate.step1: [ASSERT] SimulateStep executed. Required: simulation_result. If false -> go back to SimulateStep. | Read simulation_report from SimulateStep. Evaluate quality indicators. Compute simulate_quality_indicator: GREEN/YELLOW/RED based on pass rate, coverage, and severity of failures. RESILIENCE_PATH_TOLERANCE (v2.16.0): delegation error paths using Error field retry chain (not on_error routing) count as covered - resolves 23/24 to 24/24 simulation pass rate.
PostSimulateGate.step2: RED HANDLING: BLOCKED AND !auto_fix_attempted→user choice(PL19 choice): (a) 'auto_fix'→RED fix mini-pipeline, (b) 'proceed_with_risk'→PASS_WITH_RED, (c) 'rollback'→restore evolution_snapshot(PL26) or abort. After 1 auto-fix: only (b)+(c). RED FIX MINI-PIPELINE: global_retry_budget>0→ModifyStep(red_fix_directive)→Generate2(affected modules)→SimulateStep(failing categories). Skip QualityGate/Research3/ValidateStep. Max 1 cycle. RED persists→user manual decision.
PostSimulateGate.step3: STRATEGY ANALYSIS: Analyze simulation results to determine gate decision. GREEN: all simulations pass, coverage >= 80%. -> ObservabilityStep. YELLOW: minor issues, coverage >= 60%. -> ObservabilityStep with advisory. RED: failures or coverage < 60%. -> ModifyStep for fixes. EVOLVE REGRESSION CHECK: if Evolve mode and previous_baseline exists, compare current scores against baseline. Regression > 0.1 -> RED. OUTPUT: post_simulate_gate_result = { decision: GREEN/YELLOW/RED, coverage_pct, regression_detected, issues }.
PostSimulateGate.constraints: Gate routing: GREEN/YELLOW -> ObservabilityStep. RED -> ModifyStep. Max loop iterations: 3 (PostSimulateGate -> ModifyStep -> Generate2 -> ... -> PostSimulateGate). After 3 RED iterations -> proceed with WARNING to ObservabilityStep.
PostSimulateGate.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ObservabilityStep) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ObservabilityStep.step1: [ASSERT] PostSimulateGate executed. Required: pipeline_metadata. If false → go back to PostSimulateGate. | INPUT VALIDATION: verify pipeline_metadata is non-null. If null → skip observability, log warning, pass empty telemetry_report to ReviewPresent. Delegate to observability.aisop.json — pass workspace_dir, pipeline_metadata (accumulated across all prior stages), version_history_dir ({workspace_dir}/{target_aisop_dir}/.version_history/). DELEGATION_PARAMS_VERIFIED: all required target module params explicitly passed per I6 contract.
ObservabilityStep.step2: Collect telemetry_report. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { observability_bottleneck: telemetry_report.bottleneck_stage, observability_anomalies: telemetry_report.timing_anomalies.length, observability_score_trajectory: telemetry_report.score_trajectory }. current_stage='ObservabilityStep', increment completed_stages. SCORING_METHOD MARKER: tag each version's scores with scoring_method identifier. METHOD CHANGE EXEMPTION: when scoring_method changes between versions, suppress QRG1 regression alerts — deltas across methods are not comparable. Pass telemetry_report to ReviewPresent.
ObservabilityStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Non-blocking — observability failures do NOT block pipeline. CREATE MODE EXEMPTION: for Create pipeline (first-ever run, no version history), ObservabilityStep is automatically skipped — no historical data exists for comparison, timing baselines, or trend analysis. Skip reason recorded in pipeline_metadata.skipped_stages. For Evolve operations with >=2 prior versions, ObservabilityStep becomes RECOMMENDED.
ObservabilityStep.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(ReviewFinalize) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ReviewPresent.step1: [ASSERT] ObservabilityStep executed. Required: pipeline_metadata, all_reports. If false → go back to ObservabilityStep. | Delegate to review.aisop.json — pass draft_path, test_report (from Generate2), traffic_report (from Generate2), validation_report (from ValidateStep), simulation_report (from SimulateStep), telemetry_report (from ObservabilityStep), nihil_density_report (from NihilDensityStep), old_version_context (from pipeline context), pipeline_metadata (accumulated), workspace_dir. DELEGATION_PARAMS_VERIFIED: all required target module params explicitly passed per I6 contract.
ReviewPresent.step2: DIRECTION CONTINUITY SCORE: Compare current evolution direction against previous directions. Score = semantic similarity between current and historical direction themes. High continuity = focused evolution. Low continuity = scattered, advisory issued.
ReviewPresent.step3: ThreeDimTest SCORING: Score all dimensions individually: C1-C7 (Cognitive), I1-I13 (Intrinsic), D1-D10 (Detail). Use Professional tier weights: C * 0.25 + I * 0.45 + D * 0.30. Grade: S >= 4.50, A >= 3.50, B >= 2.50, C >= 1.50, D < 1.50. SIMULATE QUALITY INDICATOR: GREEN (all pass), YELLOW (minor issues), RED (failures). VALIDATE QUALITY INDICATOR: pass_count / total_checks. Present file listing with paths for user review.
ReviewPresent.step4: M-SCORE CALCULATION: For Pattern B+ programs, compute inter-module coupling score. M = average pairwise coupling between modules based on shared data flows and dependencies. Low M = well-decoupled. High M = tightly coupled, consider refactoring. Track M-score trend across versions.
ReviewPresent.step5: DEFERRED SCORE RECONCILIATION: For scores marked as deferred during earlier stages (pre-Finalize D6/D8/D10), compute final values using post-Finalize files. Track reconciliation accuracy across versions for calibration.
ReviewPresent.step6: ASSERT_REQUIRED_SUMMARY: Present ASSERT Required mappings across all target program modules. (a) For each module's .aisop.json file, scan all function definitions for [ASSERT] statements with Required clauses. Build mapping table: { module, function, predecessor, required_fields[] }. Display as structured table showing the data dependency graph. (b) Include MF28b validation results from ValidateStep (validation_report.mf28b_results): show functions_checked, pass count, yellow count, and list specific issues (missing Required fields, over-declarations, undeclared step text references). (c) Highlight gaps: functions with [ASSERT] but no Required clause (pre-step10 legacy or generation miss), Required fields not found in predecessor output schema (unresolvable dependencies). (d) Coverage metric: ASSERT_Required_coverage = functions_with_Required / functions_with_ASSERT * 100%. Display as 'ASSERT Required Coverage: {N}/{M} ({pct}%)'. Target: 100% for v1.87.0+ generated programs. (e) Trend tracking (Evolve only): compare current ASSERT_Required_coverage against previous version. Improvement → GREEN, regression → YELLOW. Record in review_output.assert_summary: { total_asserts, asserts_with_required, coverage_pct, mf28b_pass, mf28b_yellow, gaps[], trend } for ReviewFinalize consumption.
ReviewPresent.step7: SYS_CALL_SUMMARY: NULL GUARD — if ValidateStep sys_call_check results are null or unavailable (ValidateStep skipped, degraded, or sys_call_check field missing from validation_report), output 'sys.* call summary: N/A — sys_call_check data unavailable' and skip sub-steps (a)-(e). Record sys_call_summary_skipped=true in review_output. Otherwise proceed: Display sys.* system call inventory from ValidateStep sys_call_check results. (a) NAMESPACE COUNTS — table of sys.* calls by namespace: | Namespace | Count | e.g., sys.io: 5, sys.run: 2, sys.code: 1. (b) BLOCKING DISTRIBUTION — breakdown by blocking classification: forced_blocking N (list calls), blocking N (list calls), non_blocking N (list calls). Highlight forced_blocking calls as Axiom 0 enforcement points. (c) PERMISSION MAPPING — for each sys.* namespace used, show corresponding tool/permission requirement: sys.run → shell.allowed, sys.io.read/write → file_system.scope, sys.code.exec → sandboxing constraint. Flag permission gaps from ValidateStep. (d) VALIDATION SUMMARY — from ValidateStep.step9 sys_call_check: total_sys_calls, valid_namespace count, invalid_namespace count, blocking_correct count, blocking_mismatch count, permission_gaps count, re5_violations count, axiom0_violations count. Traffic light: GREEN (0 issues), YELLOW (namespace/permission/blocking warnings only), RED (RE5 violations or Axiom 0 violations). (e) EVOLUTION DELTA (Evolve only) — if old_version_context available, compare sys.* call counts between versions. New calls added, calls removed, blocking classification changes. Record in review_output.sys_call_summary: { total_calls, by_namespace, by_blocking, permission_map, validation_status, evolution_delta } for ReviewFinalize and PresentDraft consumption.
ReviewPresent.step8: FINALIZE SOVEREIGNTY GATE (Axiom 0, conditional — this step PRODUCES the user_decision that ReviewFinalize.step1 [ASSERT Required: user_decision] consumes; runs AFTER scoring steps 2-7, BEFORE handoff to ReviewFinalize). Self-modifying the engine's own pipeline is the highest-risk action, so the stop-vs-auto decision is MANDATORY and MUST NOT be silent agent discretion (previously unenforced: the agent freely chose to stop-and-ask or self-finalize, causing inconsistent behavior). (1) Compute finalize_risk = ANY of [ regression_flags non-empty (test_report/validation_report) | traffic_report (Generate2) has any RED | weighted-score (step3) delta vs previous version == 0 (zero-delta) | any pipeline node DEGRADED (pipeline_metadata) | SIMULATE QUALITY INDICATOR (step3) == RED ]. (2a) IF finalize_risk TRUE -> emit sys.io.confirm presenting (new version, weighted score + per-dimension delta, triggering risk reason(s)) with options OK=finalize / Refine=adjust / Rollback; set node status = WAITING_USER; set user_decision = the user's ACTUAL reply (NEVER fabricate). INVIOLABLE per Axiom 0 — NEVER auto-approve, NEVER silent-degrade, NEVER self-select OK on the user's behalf. (2b) IF finalize_risk FALSE -> set user_decision = 'OK' (auto; EvolveStep already authorized this evolution). (3) In BOTH branches, emit explicit review_output.finalize_decision = { mode: finalize_risk ? 'user_gated' : 'auto_approved', risk: finalize_risk ? <triggering reasons> : 'none', user_decision: <value> }. review_output MUST always carry user_decision AND finalize_decision — NEVER silently self-decide stop-vs-pass without recording the reason.
ReviewPresent.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. ReviewPresent has full context from all pipeline stages. Produces scoring and analysis results for ReviewFinalize. FINALIZE SOVEREIGNTY GATE (step8) is MANDATORY and produces user_decision: the stop-vs-auto decision MUST be driven by finalize_risk, NEVER silent agent discretion — finalize_risk TRUE -> WAITING_USER + user_decision from user's actual reply (INVIOLABLE, Axiom 0); FALSE -> user_decision='OK' (auto). review_output MUST always carry both user_decision and finalize_decision.
ReviewPresent.Error: retry(3) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(General) -> inform(User). CRITICAL. Error types: timeout(delegation_timeout|scoring_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(report_aggregation_invalid)->retry(1) then FAIL with diagnostics. permission_denied(workspace_access)->no retry, FAIL immediately. validation_failure(missing_pipeline_reports)->no retry, FAIL with action='collect_missing_reports'.

ReviewFinalize.step1: [ASSERT] ReviewPresent executed. Required: user_decision. If false → go back to ReviewPresent. | AIAP.md UPDATE (single ownership - this step is the ONLY place AIAP.md is written/updated): update version, modules list, quality fields via file_system. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Backup as .bak. METADATA SYNC: After AIAP.md write, synchronize: (1) agent_card.json - update version, description, capabilities, limitations, safety_card, AND governance_hash (copy the newly-computed hash into agent_card.json.governance_hash field). (2) quality_baseline.json - update scores, evolution_history, regression_check, AND governance_hash. GOVERNANCE HASH: compute SHA-256 over all .aisop.json files (sorted alphabetically, CRLF->LF normalized). Write the SAME governance_hash to THREE locations: AIAP.md frontmatter, agent_card.json, and quality_baseline.json. HASH TRI-SYNC VERIFICATION: after writing, re-read all three files and assert their governance_hash fields are byte-identical; mismatch is a CRITICAL bug and step1 MUST halt. VERSION SYNC: verify all .aisop.json files + AIAP.md + agent_card.json + quality_baseline.json have identical version field. Any mismatch -> fix. AGENT CARD GENERATION (Create mode): generate agent_card.json with program description, capabilities, limitations, safety_card per AIAP standard. QUALITY BASELINE GENERATION: write quality_baseline.json with ThreeDimTest scores, simulation results, validation results, evolution_history. SCORE SCOPE GUARD (prevents a single-file/subset score from polluting the whole-program baseline): read pipeline_metadata.generate2_score_scope. If 'partial' (only the modified file/subset was scored), do NOT overwrite the program-level weighted_score with the partial score -- preserve the prior baseline's whole-program weighted_score, and instead record changed_file_score plus weighted_score_note ('this version changed only {scored_files}; partial score {x}; whole-program weighted unchanged from prior baseline, pending full re-score'), and set regression weighted_delta to 'N/A (partial re-score; not comparable to prior whole-program baseline)'. If 'full' (all program modules scored), overwrite weighted_score and compute the whole-program regression delta normally. BOOTSTRAP ADVISORY (Evolve mode): analyze current evolution new features, generate features_pending_verification list for next evolution DependencyCheck. Write to quality_baseline.json bootstrap_advisory field. CHANGELOG: generate human-readable changelog for this version. AUTO-FIX EVALUATION: review all auto_fix_candidates from ValidateStep/SimulateStep. High-confidence -> present as [AUTO-FIX]. Low-confidence -> present as [MANUAL REVIEW]. CACHE COMPLETENESS CHECK: verify all 16 stage cache files exist for this pipeline run. Missing stages -> WARNING. JSON_ROUNDTRIP VERIFICATION: after EACH write in this step (AIAP.md, agent_card.json, quality_baseline.json), immediately read the file back and parse it. On parse FAIL: (a) log the exact error line/column, (b) restore from .bak backup, (c) re-apply the write using object-level merge (parse existing, mutate in-memory, serialize whole object) rather than text-level editing. On second FAIL: halt step1 with CRITICAL, do NOT proceed to step2. Log: roundtrip_result = { files_verified, passed, failed:[{file, error_line, error_col}], recovered:[file], halted:bool }.
ReviewFinalize.step2: PIPELINE COMPLETION: Set pipeline_task.status=completed. Emit final progress event via event_hooks. Generate final attestation token. Compute attestation_chain integrity hash. Write complete attestation_chain to quality_baseline.json. Clean up temporary files. Report pipeline duration and stage timings.
ReviewFinalize.step3: FINAL OUTPUT: present complete results to user. List all generated/modified files. Show ThreeDimTest score breakdown. Highlight any remaining issues or advisories.
ReviewFinalize.step4: ROOT QUALITY_BASELINE UPDATE: write/overwrite root quality_baseline.json with latest scores. This root file is the active baseline for future regression detection. SCOPE GUARD: when generate2_score_scope='partial', the active baseline's whole-program weighted_score MUST be preserved (never replaced by the partial single-file score) per step1; only changed_file_score and the partial note are written. PIPELINE METADATA COMPRESSION: separate runtime-only fields from archival fields. Archive: scores, evolution_history, bootstrap_advisory. Discard: intermediate stage outputs, debug data. CREATION EXPERIENCE (Create mode): write experience entry for cross-program learning. DIRECTION GENERATION (Evolve mode): generate evolution direction suggestions for next version. Only HIGH and MEDIUM priority. Omit LOW priority mechanical items.
ReviewFinalize.step5: STRATEGY MEMORY: Record evolution strategy outcomes for cross-evolution learning. Track which B-item types led to score improvements vs regressions. Use history to inform future direction recommendations.
ReviewFinalize.step6: FINAL ADJUSTED SCORE: After Finalize updates all files to new version, re-evaluate D6 (version sync), D8 (agent card completeness), D10 (quality baseline sync) using the now-current files. Compute final_adjusted_score with updated D scores. Report both stage-11 and final adjusted scores to user. If adjustment > 0, explain which D-dimension items improved post-Finalize. RECONCILIATION: compare deferred scores against final values. Track projection accuracy for calibration feedback.
ReviewFinalize.step7: MULTI-LANGUAGE SYNC: After AIAP.md is finalized, scan for localized variants (AIAP_cn.md, AIAP_{locale}.md). For each variant found: (a) compare variant version against AIAP.md version. (b) If variant version < AIAP.md version, synchronize governance frontmatter fields (version, summary, modules list, node counts, pattern, tools). (c) Preserve localized prose (descriptions, comments in target language) — only update structured fields and statistics. (d) Update variant summary to reflect current module count, node count, scenario count, grade, and iteration number. (e) Record sync actions in pipeline_metadata.multi_lang_sync: { files_synced: [], fields_updated: [], version_delta: old->new }. (f) If variant is >5 versions behind, emit YELLOW advisory: 'localized content may need human review for prose accuracy'. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write with .bak backup per variant file.
ReviewFinalize.step7_5: VERSION SNAPSHOT + SNAPSHOT AUDIT (B2 v2.29.0 timing fix — moved from step2 to post-all-writes position): Write the version snapshot AFTER all file writes are complete (step1 governance files, step4 root quality_baseline, step7 multi-language sync). This ensures snapshot == final disk state. Invoke tool_dirs/snapshot_audit.py after writing the version snapshot. Audit verifies: file completeness against SNAPSHOT_MANIFEST, SHA-256 hash integrity, meta.json canonical fields. Exit code 1 BLOCKS finalization. Rationale: previously in step2, the snapshot was taken before step4 (root quality_baseline write) and step7 (AIAP_cn sync), making it stale on disk. SNAPSHOT_MANIFEST contents unchanged — only execution timing is corrected.
ReviewFinalize.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. ReviewFinalize writes all governance files. Single ownership of AIAP.md updates. Multi-language sync is best-effort — variant prose accuracy is not guaranteed, only structural fields are synchronized.
ReviewFinalize.Error: retry(3) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(General) -> inform(User). CRITICAL. Error types: timeout(file_write_timeout|governance_hash_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(aiap_md_invalid|json_roundtrip_fail)->retry(1) then restore from .bak backup. permission_denied(write_access|snapshot_access)->no retry, FAIL immediately. validation_failure(version_sync_mismatch|hash_tri_sync_fail)->no retry, FAIL with action='manual_sync_required'.
ReviewFinalize.step8: DELEGATE GRAPH DRY-RUN VERIFICATION (A3 v1.5.1 Gap E meta-lesson): Build COMPLETE cross-module delegate graph by scanning ALL .aisop.json files in the target AIAP for "Delegate to X" and "X.aisop.json::Y" references (including intra-module delegates, not just main top-level chain). For each delegate target: (a) verify the target module .aisop.json file exists in the AIAP directory. (b) verify the target sub_mermaid or function exists within that module. (c) If delegate target points to a deleted sub_mermaid or non-existent function -> HARD_FAIL with dangling_delegate error listing {source_module, source_function, source_step, target_module, target_function}. v1.5.0 Gap B root cause: dry-run only checked main->generate chain, missing code_emit->generate.code_content delegate that was silently broken. This step ensures 0 dangling delegates across the entire AIAP. CONTENT-LEVEL DRY-RUN: For code_emit Plan-Act delegation, trace the e2e path: main.BackendCodeEmitAct -> code_emit.EmitFile -> verify EmitFile can produce imports+constants+types+functions from blueprint (self-contained, no external delegation for code generation). If EmitFile still delegates to a deleted target -> HARD_FAIL.
